resources.string: Variables: {name} = input filename without extension, {format} = output format (cia/cci/3ds). The extension is added automatically.
About %1$s available, but the decrypt may need around %2$s. Continue anyway?
That is a .zip. Use the "Choose a .zip file" button instead.
Saved as .cia (cannot rebuild as .%1$s for this title type).
.cia: original CIA package, decrypted in place. Safest for emulator install.
.cci: raw NCSD cartridge image. Smaller file; only valid for game CIAs.
.3ds: same NCSD bytes as .cci, with the extension some emulators expect.
Pick an output folder to continue.
No .cia, .3ds or .zip files found in this folder.
Files go directly into the folder you pick.
Files go into a new subfolder named after the .zip.
Non-game files (DLC, patches, system) will be saved as .cia regardless of the selected format.
Open-source on-device decryptor for Nintendo 3DS .cia and .3ds files.
Built on ctrtool and makerom by 3DSGuy/Project_CTR (MIT). seeddb.bin maintained by ihavamac. Original Windows tool by matiffeder; redux by xxmichibxx.

cia3ds-android is released under the MIT License. You are free to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and sell copies of the Software, provided the copyright notice and this permission notice appear in all copies. The software is provided \"as is\" with no warranty.

• ctrtool, makerom (3DSGuy/Project_CTR): MIT
• libtoolchain, libfmt, libnintendo-n3ds, libbroadon-es: MIT
• libblz, libyaml: permissive (MIT-style)
• mbedTLS 2.x: Apache-2.0
• Bundled seeddb.bin: community-maintained (ihavamac and contributors)
This file appears to already be decrypted, so there is nothing to do. You can install it directly in your emulator, or pick a different (still-encrypted) CIA/3DS.
